Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Theme post: Insights Launch repurpose (measurement)
Description:
Topic: Theme post — repurpose a stat/moment from last week's Insights Update launch into a measurement-themed LinkedIn post; soft tie to the 7/9 webinar.
Post type: static-theme
Wraps: — (theme: Insights launch repurpose / measurement)
Copy:
Every internal comms tool now says it does measurement. Watch the demos and they all land on the same screen: a dashboard of percentages. Open rate. Click rate. A number that went up or down since last quarter.

A percentage tells you something happened to a crowd. It cannot tell you it reached the person who needed it.

Here is the screen that changes the conversation. In a demo of Cerkl's new Insights last week, our team pulled up a single link in a single send and saw, by name, exactly who clicked it. Not a percentage. Three people: two we expected, one we didn't. Then the inverse: the named list of who got the message and never opened it.

That is the unit of measurement IC has been missing. Not "the update performed well." "These eleven managers on the night shift never saw it, and here they are."

The same view runs on a pulse survey or an acknowledgment. Filter to the people who said yes, and to the people who said nothing, then send only to the second group.

Aggregate rates are for the board deck. Named attribution is for the work.

Stop Guessing About Your Analytics — Rachel Folz + Maddy Rieman, July 9. Link in comments.

Asset: Named-attribution card. Top, smaller/grayed (category default): faint tile "Click rate: 14%". Below, larger/on-brand: a short roster table — "Carly: clicked / Jordan: clicked / Maria: did not open / +11 did not open". Headline: "Measurement isn't a percentage. It's a name." Cerkl brand colors; use GENERIC first names on the final graphic. Text-only, no photo; needs origination (no existing manifest).

#InternalCommunications #EmployeeEngagement #ICMeasurement

First comment: Register for "Stop Guessing About Your Analytics" (7/9, Rachel Folz + Maddy Rieman): https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_-vu4-AcITfGJq0KEHysSSw

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Link post: Stop Guessing About Your Analytics (webinar pre-event blog)
Description:
Topic: Link post — amplify the webinar pre-event blog (the real 5 questions). Drive blog reads + webinar registration.
Post type: static-blog
Wraps: internal-communications-analytics-questions
Copy:
You send the all-staff message about the reorg. It goes clean to 240 inboxes. Then your VP asks, "Did people read it?" and you don't know that the one director whose team is most affected never opened it.

That gap between "we sent it" and "we can prove who engaged" is a measurement problem, not an effort problem. Gmail and Outlook were built to move external email, not to tell you whether your own employees engaged with it.

Five questions every internal communicator should be able to answer about any message:
1. Who received the message?
2. Who is engaged and who isn't?
3. When should I send for maximum impact?
4. What content actually resonates with employees?
5. What should I improve next?

Question 2 separates a real measurement tool from a vanity dashboard. A percentage tells you something is wrong. A name tells you what to do about it. When you can see that Dana in the Cleveland warehouse and eleven colleagues missed the benefits reminder three days before the deadline, that's a follow-up you can still make.

The blog walks through all five. Rachel Folz and Maddy Rieman go deeper on July 9 in our free webinar, "Stop Guessing About Your Analytics," with the new Insights experience shown live.

Here's the part most communicators don't believe yet: you can answer all five yourself, no analytics skills required. Leadership-ready reporting is built into Foundations, free.

Read the five questions: [LIVE BLOG URL — Furqan inserts at publish]
Register for July 9: https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_-vu4-AcITfGJq0KEHysSSw

Asset: Link card — LinkedIn auto-renders from the blog URL once Furqan inserts it. No Canva render.

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Poll: Can you answer these 5 questions about your last send?
Description:
Topic: Poll — mirrors the webinar's live poll #2 ("first metric leadership asks for"). Drive engagement + webinar registration.
Post type: poll
Wraps: internal-communications-measurement-framework
Copy:
POLL QUESTION (native widget): What's the first metric leadership asks you for after a major communication?
OPTIONS: 1) Open rate  2) Reach & delivery  3) Clicks  4) Something else
Duration: 1 week

CAPTION:
Think about your last all-staff send. Can you name a single employee who read it?

Most internal comms teams can report an open rate. Far fewer can answer the question leadership asks next: did the people who needed this message read it?

That gap is a maturity-stage problem, not a discipline problem. The teams that move past it get to per-employee read attribution: not an average, but a named list. (Illustrative: "94% of affected employees read the policy change in 48 hours, and here's the 6% who didn't, by department.")

So here's the self-diagnosis. When leadership comes to you after a big send, what do they ask for first?

We're running this exact poll live at our 7/9 webinar, "Stop Guessing About Your Analytics," with Rachel Folz and Maddy Rieman. Stop guessing whether your comms work. And yes, you can do this yourself. Registration in comments.

#InternalCommunications #EmployeeEngagement #HRTech

Copy:
We just shipped Insights, a ground-up rebuild of Broadcast's analytics.

Here is the problem it solves. A 60% open rate tells you almost nothing. It hides which teams read your message and which never saw it, whether managers engaged or only HR did, which locations are tuned out. The average is the story leadership asks about, and it buries what you actually need to know.

Insights was built for communicators, not analysts: see engagement down to the named person, compare groups, and turn it into an answer instead of a guess.

On Thursday July 9 at 12pm EDT, Rachel Folz (Head of Product) and Maddy Rieman (Head of Customer Success) run it live: "Stop Guessing About Your Analytics: 5 Questions Every Internal Communicator Should Answer," walking through each question with the new reporting on screen.

Free in Foundations. Register here: https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/2617762528258/WN_-vu4-AcITfGJq0KEHysSSw
